rationale: In the citing paper, Zhu, Billeter, and Inman (2012) appears only as part of a grouped parenthetical citation supporting the general claim that product efficacy perceptions are malleable. The paper does not single out this study's specific findings, mechanism, or design when developing hypotheses or interpreting results. The citing article's core theory and tests focus on sharing-out vs. sharing-in and product identification, so this reference functions as broad background context rather than a distinct building block.
